structure.adaptive_details: Two-stage adaptive testing. Performance on Module 1 determines the difficulty of Module 2 (routing).
Two-stage adaptive testing. Performance on Module 1 determines the difficulty of Module 2 (routing).

Reading and Writing.topics: Words in Context, Text Structure and Purpose, Cross-Text Connections
Central Ideas and Details, Command of Evidence, Inferences

Mathematics.topics: Linear Equations in One Variable, Linear Equations in Two Variables, Linear Functions, Systems of Two Linear Equations, Linear Inequalities in One or Two Variables
Equivalent Expressions, Nonlinear Equations in One Variable and Systems of Equations in Two Variables, Nonlinear Functions
Ratios, Rates, Proportional Relationships, and Units, Percentages, One-Variable Data, Two-Variable Data, Probability, Inference from Sample Data, Evaluating Statistical Claims
Area and Volume, Lines, Angles, and Triangles, Right Triangles and Trigonometry, Circles

scoring.methodology: Item Response Theory (IRT). Question difficulties are weighted, no penalty for guessing.

sat_digital.calculator_policy: Graphing calculator allowed on all math sections. Built-in Desmos calculator is provided.
sat_digital.formula_sheet: Reference formulas provided for geometry: area of circle/triangle/rectangle, volume of sphere/cone/cylinder/prism, special right triangle ratios, circle degrees/radians.

structure.description: Assesses standard English conventions, grammar, usage, and rhetorical skills.
Covers high school math subjects with four-option multiple choice questions.
Comprehend and analyze literary and informational texts with integrated visual data.
Assesses interpretation, analysis, and reasoning using scientific experiments and viewpoints.
One essay topic requesting evaluation of multiple perspectives on an issue.

English.topics: Grammar, Usage, and Mechanics, Sentence Structure, Punctuation
Topic Development, Organization, Unity, and Cohesion

Mathematics.topics: Number & Quantity, Algebra, Functions, Geometry, Statistics & Probability
Ratios, Percentages, and Proportions, Basic Area and Volume, Fractions and Decimals

Reading.topics: Word Meaning in Context, Text Structure, Author's Purpose
Analyzing Claims and Evidence, Integrating Multiple Texts, Visual/Quantitative Data Integration

Science.topics: Reading Trends, Analyzing Tables and Graphs

Science.domain: Evaluation of Models, Inferences, and Experimental Results
Science.topics: Judging Validity of Models, Formulating Conclusions

Writing.topics: Ideas and Analysis, Development and Support, Organization, Language Use and Conventions

scoring.scale: 1-36 composite (average of English, Math, and Reading). Essay scored 2-12.
scoring.methodology: Number of correct answers converted to scale score. No penalty for guessing.

act_enhanced_2025.calculator_policy: Permitted on Mathematics section. Approved calculators only (no CAS features unless disabled).

act_classic_pdf.note: Preparing for the ACT 2024-25 uses classic timing
